posts.text:
BREAKING NEWS - 100% of GOP voters who self identify by Trump's campaign slogan approve of Trump. 

Thank you Harry Enten and CNN, this is very important and insightful news.


Karoline Leavitt (@PressSec)

CNN:

“MAGA GOP view of Trump, approve is 100%. If you are a member of MAGA in the GOP, you approve of Donald John Trump. 0% say that they disapprove… he’s the 1972 Miami Dolphins.”

Video
  





— https://nitter.net/PressSec/status/2034302387523883128#m

This will be the pretense for the White House trying to negate the results of the midterms, by the way.


Katie Miller (@KatieMiller)

We don’t live in a Democracy if we don’t have integrity in our elections.



— https://nitter.net/KatieMiller/status/2034177721371127847#m

I rooted for Team USA in the WBC, but they played a joyless tournament, and the military cosplay (salutes, Rob O'Neill locker room visit, etc) was ridiculous. Also, Aaron Judge may be the least clutch player to have played the game. Congratulations to Venezuela, they earned it.
This specific situation aside, most people would be shocked if they knew how much of their media/digital spend is taken up in various mostly hidden (to them) fees. This needs to stop.

If you're a Dem campaign or progressive org, you should know if your digital agency is using this DSP that allegedly just failed a client audit based on the extra fees they were charging the client. adweek.com/programmatic/publ…

Imagine if the party in power put 1/10th of the energy they are putting behind this voter suppression bill to things Americans actually cared about, such as lowering prices or ending wars.


Rep. Anna Paulina Luna (@RepLuna)

The American people are SICK of performative politics. We don’t want a SHOW VOTE we want RESULTS! We want the SAVE America Act delivered to the President’s desk.

Video
  





— https://nitter.net/RepLuna/status/2033723363604971818#m

Not a dry eye in the house, I'm sure. Baseball is so joyful and we love America, and especially war stuff.


Sammy James🔺 (@HebHammer94)

At Dunkin just now, I noticed the staff looking a little tired so I started LOUDLY playing Robert O'Neill's Team USA speech about killing Bin Laden. By the time I left, the staff thanked me, shook my hand and recited the Pledge of Allegiance as I exited the store. Incredible 🇱🇷



— https://nitter.net/HebHammer94/status/2032826409219928389#m

I'm incredibly grateful towards those who serve our country, but why is it that every Team USA has to perform military cosplay? You're playing a game for our collective entertainment. AFAIK our baseball team didn't invade Canada or anything.


Jomboy Media (@JomboyMedia)

Robert J. O’Neill spoke in Team USA’s clubhouse ahead of their game vs. Canada

O’Neill was one of the members of SEAL Team Six that carried out the mission to kill Osama bin Laden

Video
  





— https://nitter.net/JomboyMedi

Because it's already illegal, dipshit.


Nick Sortor (@nicksortor)

🚨 JUST IN: JD Vance absolutely DEMOLISHES one of the Democrats' key arguments against the SAVE America Act

"Even if you take the Democrats at their word—even if illegal aliens voting is very rare, then why don't we BAN it anyway? It's NOT that complicated."

"I don't get it! The Democrats say 'this never happens' or 'it only happens very rarely!' But then they say 'we must not stop it!'"

"It doesn't make a damn bit of sense! W

Coming up shortly!


Lincoln Square (@LincolnSquareHQ)

Join us for "It's the Democracy, Stupid" today at 1pm ET! @eisendrath is joined by veteran Democratic strategist  @tbonier to discuss the data, and what the midterms look like for Democrats and more. Subscribe for free to watch:
open.substack.com/live-strea…





— https://nitter.net/LincolnSquareHQ/status/2032483239609188708#m

Republicans are so terrified of losing, they've elevated this voter suppression bill above and beyond any legislative action designed to actually improve the lives of Americans, and will stop at nothing to implement it.


Jake Sherman (@JakeSherman)

CORNYN has a new op ed saying the SAVE America Act is more important than the filibuster. 

“For many years, I believed that if the US Senate scrapped the filibuster, Texas and our nation would stand to lose more than we would gain.

“My fellow cons

What's more, the fact that Dem share of the primary turnout in Hispanic counties was so massive is clearly a strong indicator that Hispanics swung back to Dems. If you can't see how that might impact the general election, I cannot help you.

Sometimes it's not that complicated. Like in the case of Zapata County, TX where 2,700 Dem votes were cast in the primary. That's more votes than Harris, or Beto x2, or Biden won in their November general elections. Historically, almost every primary voter will vote in November.

If someone wants to make a t-shirt of this, I will buy many.


Governor Maura Healey (@MassGovernor)







— https://nitter.net/MassGovernor/status/2029204859019313342#m

Important context as to the “how” behind the incredible numbers from Texas I’ve been sharing this morning.


Chuck Rocha (@ChuckRocha)

The facts about how @TeamTalaricoHQ won last night

Video
  





— https://nitter.net/ChuckRocha/status/2029193039214690600#m

It’s hard to overstate how rare it is to see Dem turnout in a midterm primary election exceeding that of a presidential election.


Tom Bonier (@tbonier)

Wow, the Dem primary turnout among Hispanic voters in Texas is going to be giving the GOP sweats for the next 8 months. 

Take Zapata County - turnout in the Dem primary there yesterday was 143% the total number of votes Harris won in the 2024 general election. That's wild.



— https://nitter.net/tbonier/status/2029162284942024705#m

Not every vote is in (the outlined counties don't have >95% counted, and Maverick hasn't reported at all), but this shows the swing between Harris' margin in '24 and the Dem margin in yesterday's primary turnout. You can see in south Texas the shifts were >100%.
Let's keep in mind, the GOP reportedly spent upwards of $80 million on their primaries in Texas, well more than double the amount spent by Dems.
Here are 4 districts the TX GOP gerrymandered to gain seats in November, with the Dem and GOP primary vote. Dems outvoted GOPs in every one of them.

Wow, the Dem primary turnout among Hispanic voters in Texas is going to be giving the GOP sweats for the next 8 months. 

Take Zapata County - turnout in the Dem primary there yesterday was 143% the total number of votes Harris won in the 2024 general election. That's wild.

Join us for "It's the Democracy, Stupid" today at 1pm ET! @eisendrath.net is joined by veteran Democratic strategist  @tbonier.bsky.social to discuss data, democracy and what the midterms look like for Democrats and more. Subscribe for free to watch:
open.substack.com/live-stream/...

Not every vote is in (the outlined counties don't have >95% counted, and Maverick hasn't reported at all), but this shows the swing between Harris' margin in '24 and the Dem margin in yesterday's primary turnout. You can see in south Texas the shifts were >100%.
Let's keep in mind, the GOP reportedly spent upwards of $80 million on their primaries in Texas, well more than double the amount spent by Dems.
Here are 4 districts the TX GOP gerrymandered to gain seats in November, with the Dem and GOP primary vote. Dems outvoted GOPs in every one of them.

Wow, the Dem primary turnout among Hispanic voters in Texas is going to be giving the GOP sweats for the next 8 months. 

Take Zapata County - turnout in the Dem primary there yesterday was 143% the total number of votes Harris won in the 2024 general election. That's wild.
There are only 348 voters in the cell so I wouldn’t read too much into it.
Here's a better version with it sorted by partisanship gaps, and with the N size included for each group

Some notes:
- Black women are leading the way with the biggest Dem advantage across every age group
- Black men are also showing huge Dem margins
- Dems lead in every single group of under age 30 voters, even white men
- GOPs don't have an advantage with any group <50

I made this monster of a chart to show the partisan split of the early/abs vote in the TX primary by age/race/gender. 

You can see why Republicans must be worried about what they're seeing in the turnout data.

A short thread on letting go of Zombie, pre-Trump 2025-2026 understanding of politics......

We are in a new era.  Strategists must stay close to the events of today, to the data in front of us, and not bring old understandings to cloud or hamper with what must be done now.  1/
Republicans seem to in denial of the wild unpopularity of their actions, and the looming political disaster they are speedwalking into.
In 2020 GOP turnout exceeded Dem by 7.1%, and by 6.4% in 2022 and 2024. Yet in 2025, Dem turnout in PA exceed GOP turnout by 8%. That's a swing of close to 15 pts in favor of Dems, just in turnout.

We have the turnout data now from the 2025 election in PA that saw the Dems sweep the statewide judicial races, as well as downballot races. 

Dem turnout swamped a depressed GOP base. If I were GOPs, I'd be more than a little worried about the upcoming midterms.
I have to assume it is some combination of lack of mobility and higher death rates (it can take some time for people to be purged from the voter rolls).
As far as total turnout by party,  here are the differentials from 2021 to 2025. GOP turnout actually increased over 2021, but only slightly. Dem turnout saw a massive increase of 14.7 pts.
There was a massive youth vote surge in NJ this year. In 2021 voters under 30 accounted for 7.1% of all votes cast. This year they were 11.5% of the electorate.
Look at turnout by age/party in New Jersey - Dems outperformed GOPs in turnout % among every age <45, with the biggest advantage among 21 and 22 year olds (+10% Dem turnout).
Past special elections with massive swings to Dems could be explained away by GOPs, because turnout was relatively low. Not this one. Republicans should be deeply concerned.

There you have it. With Davidson County reporting every precinct now, TN7 turnout is at 179,634, just barely short of the 181,822 turnout in the 2022 general election. I wouldn't be surprised if it exceeds it when all votes are counted.
